published_prior_art.cite: rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation gives sequential convex programming, not a laminar TU/min-cost-flow certificate

gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds is one-cutoff; bertanha_2020_many_thresholds and cattaneo_keele_titiunik_vazquezbare_2021_multicutoff are many-cutoff point-ID/extrapolation, not manipulation PartialID computation

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded/v1: ratio-indexed one-cutoff retained-plan interval without explicit many-neighborhood slice dual

published_prior_art.cite: orlin_1993_mincostflow supplies generic min-cost-flow duality/algorithms; no causal RD manipulation slice certificate
gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds and lee_2009_selection_bounds cover the one-cutoff/trimming ancestry

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The headline promises a grid-expanded min-cost-flow certificate, but Exhibit 9.1 does not instantiate the actual node set, arc set, capacities, balances, or min-cost-flow solution from the Section 6 primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The dual certificate object is promised as (alpha,beta,gamma,delta), but Exhibit 9.1 gives only the objective equality and no numeric potentials, reduced-cost slacks, or arc-by-arc complementary-slackness audit.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation upgrade is partial: the ratio table and boundary case are explicit, but the declared flow/dual certificate delta in the delta_summary is not yet exhibited; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The fixed-slice dual is written for an unnamed graph with unspecified node balances and arc inventory, so the certificate cannot yet be independently audited from the stated primitives.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a full Exhibit 9.2 for the J=2 worked instance listing V, E, u, b, one optimal flow f, numeric node potentials pi, numeric slacks s, and the complementary-slackness checks.
State the construction of the laminar network before the dual: source/atom/demand/carry nodes, every arc family, capacities, balances, and how nested extendability is encoded.
Separate the proven fixed-slice TU theorem from the open strong-polynomial search over Mext by adding a small explicit optimization-oracle subproblem for the worked instance.

recommended_next_step: Revise Section 3 and Exhibit 9.1 so the promised lifted flow and dual certificate are computed explicitly from primitives, then resubmit.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal improves the parent with candidate ratios and a worked endpoint table, but has not yet delivered the advertised computable flow/dual certificate as an explicit object.
